title: koeleria cristata subsp. askoldensis
intro: Koeleria cristata subsp. askoldensis, also known as Askold's Koeleria, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to certain regions and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function, due to its rich content of bioactive compounds. Koeleria cristata subsp. askoldensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
